question.text: What powers does the Director of FIU-IND have under Section 13(2) of PMLA?

answer.text: Power to impose fines on banking companies, financial institutions or intermediaries for failing to maintain records or furnish information
Power to arrest persons suspected of money laundering
Power to freeze all assets of any financial institution
Power to revoke the license of any bank

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under Section 13(2) of PMLA, the Director of FIU-IND may impose a fine on any banking company, financial institution, or intermediary for failing to comply with obligations of maintenance of records or to furnish information or to verify identities of clients. **The Trap:** FIU-IND Director has civil court-like powers under Section 13 but cannot arrest persons (that's enforcement directorate's power) or revoke bank licenses (RBI's power). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The Director of FIU-IND has the same powers as a civil court under CPC 1908 for Section 13 proceedings, including compelling production of records and discovery.
